title: Elden Ring
verdict: The crossroads themselves, made flesh. I tended every torch.
review:
Some games are met. This one was inhabited.
The Lands Between asked for everything — patience, ruin, and the willingness to die at the same wall a hundred times. I gave it all of that.
What I carried out was not just a finished credits screen but a kind of reverence. The dark here is beautiful, and it does not lie to you. Conquered, fully, and gladly.

title: Hollow Knight
verdict: Descended into the dark and came back lit.
review:
A hollow kingdom, drawn in ink and silence.
Every corridor felt older than the last. The further down I went, the more it became clear the game was never going to hold my hand — only hand me a nail and a guttering lantern.
I conquered it. The silence stays with me.

title: Starfield
verdict: A thousand worlds, none of them haunted. Left to the dark.
review:
I went looking for a crossroads and found a menu.
There was nothing wrong with it, exactly — and that was the problem. A thousand worlds, all loading screens and no weight, none of them willing to meet me in the dark.
I set down the torch and walked away. Some things are not conquered. They are simply left.
